other_observers: None on 12/30/2013; Peter Burke showed me his photo of the same individual taken in Boulder County on 12/31/2013. Observed independently by Cole WIld and Cade Cropper on the morning of 1/1/2014 at Carter Lake in Larimer; observed with me by Elena Klaver, Topi Martinez, Linda Andes-Georges and Rose Watson at the Larimer Landfill mid-morning on 1/1/2014; observed with me by David Bray at Carter Lake on the afternoon of 1/2/2014.
description: First observed in flight, noting lack of noticeable dark markings on the underwing. Then, sitting (seen through a scope), I noticed intermediate size betweeen Ring-billed Gull and American Herring Gull, lack of brown streaking on head except for lower hindneck, gray of saddle paler than that of Ring-billed and Herring gulls; darker gray wingtips with large white terminal spots on primaries, such that on one side (the bird's right side) where the primaries were well-folded over each other, it was difficult to discern the darker gray of the wing-tip at all. On the bird's left side, several inches of darker gray of the outer webs of the outer primaries was evident below the large white tips of each of the outer primaries. The underparts and tail were entirely white. The bill was yellow with a greenish wash and a small red gonys spot. The size of the bill was not much bigger than the bills of nearby Ring-billed Gulls. The eye was yellow, but not as bright as that of Herring, Ring-billed or Lesser Black-backed Gull. It was paler than that of California Gulls, and most Thayer's Gulls. In flight the wingtips appeared completely white, although in photos the gray was apparent, along with large white mirrors on the outer 4 primaries.
photographer: Photographer: N. Komar, N. Komar, N. Komar, N. Komar, N. Komar
